body_txt: Describe No, You here. No, ''You'' describe No, You here! ... Strange. The act of replying to an insult by simply saying "No, you!" or something similar, meaning that said insult applies more to the person giving it than the person receiving it. Omnipresent in schools, especially middle to high schools. Among adults, it's generally considered to be a fairly weak riposte , though in some cases it may still manage to be humorous (if only due to the speaker's sheer lack of wit and creativity or responding to an innocuous statement with a random insult). Tends to have more heft to it when the original insult in question genuinely applies more to the person giving it. Similar comebacks: Sometimes this is used as a Pre-Asskicking One-Liner or a Shut Up, Hannibal! . May overlap with Verbal Deflection . Compare "May Be X, But Our X" Remark . See also "You!" Exclamation , Lame Comeback , Big, Stupid Doodoo-Head , I Will Show You X! , Argument of Contradictions , [Verb] This! , Volleying Insults , At Least I Admit It .
